heading: 9.7.Instructions (part 87)
content:
A warp executing sparse `mma.m16n8k128` with `.u4` / `.s4` / `.e2m1` integer type will compute an MMA
operation of shape `.m16n8k128`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u4` / `.s4` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight non-zero `.u4` / `.s4` elements out of 16 consecutive elements from matrix A. | Mapping of the non-zero elements is as described in [Sparse matrix storage](#warp-level-sparse-matrix-storage). |
  | `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight non-zero `.e2m1` elements out of 16 consecutive elements from matrix A. |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 140](#sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-a-first64col)
  and [Figure 141](#sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-a-last64col).

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-A-first64col]
SPARSE MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
----------------------------------
Instruction: sparse MMA .m16n8k128
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), or .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
This figure covers columns 0 through 63 of matrix A (the left half of the K=128 dimension).
Full matrix A dimensions: 16 rows x 128 columns (K dimension), but this figure shows columns 0-63.

2. GRID STRUCTURE
-----------------
Rows (matrix rows): 0 through 15 (16 rows total)
Columns (matrix columns shown): 0-15, 16-31, 32-47, 48-63 (four column groups, each 16 columns wide)
Threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)

3. COMPLETE ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-TO-REGISTER MAPPING
---------------------------------------------------

ROWS 0-7 (upper half of matrix A), columns 0-63:

Row 0:
  Columns 0-15:   T0:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 16-31:  T1:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 32-47:  T2:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 48-63:  T3:{a0 ... a7}

Row 1:
  Columns 0-15:   T4:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 16-31:  T5:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 32-47:  T6:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 48-63:  T7:{a0 ... a7}

Row 2:
  (implied continuation of pattern, cells blank in figure)
  Columns 0-15:   T8:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 16-31:  T9:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 32-47:  T10:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 48-63:  T11:{a0 ... a7}

Rows 3-6: (blank in figure, pattern continues with T12-T27 in groups of 4 threads per row)

Row 7:
  Columns 0-15:   T28:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 16-31:  T29:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 32-47:  T30:{a0 ... a7}
  Columns 48-63:  T31:{a0 ... a7}

ROWS 8-15 (lower half of matrix A), columns 0-63:

Row 8:
  Columns 0-15:   T0:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 16-31:  T1:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 32-47:  T2:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 48-63:  T3:{a8 ... a15}

Row 9:
  Columns 0-15:   T4:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 16-31:  T5:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 32-47:  T6:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 48-63:  T7:{a8 ... a15}

Row 10:
  (implied continuation, cells blank in figure)
  Columns 0-15:   T8:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 16-31:  T9:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 32-47:  T10:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 48-63:  T11:{a8 ... a15}

Rows 11-14: (blank in figure, pattern continues with T12-T27)

Row 15:
  Columns 0-15:   T28:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 16-31:  T29:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 32-47:  T30:{a8 ... a15}
  Columns 48-63:  T31:{a8 ... a15}

4. DATA TYPE
------------
.u4 (unsigned 4-bit integer), .s4 (signed 4-bit integer), or .e2m1 (floating point with 2-bit exponent and 1-bit mantissa)
All three types share this same fragment layout.

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
---------------------
Thread-to-row mapping (rows 0-7):
  Each matrix row (0 through 7) is handled by exactly 4 consecutive threads.
  Row 0  -> T0, T1, T2, T3
  Row 1  -> T4, T5, T6, T7
  Row 2  -> T8, T9, T10, T11
  Row 3  -> T12, T13, T14, T15
  Row 4  -> T16, T17, T18, T19
  Row 5  -> T20, T21, T22, T23
  Row 6  -> T24, T25, T26, T27
  Row 7  -> T28, T29, T30, T31

Thread-to-column mapping (columns 0-63):
  Within each row, the 4 threads cover 4 column groups of 16 columns each:
  Thread offset 0 (e.g., T0, T4, T8, ...) -> columns 0-15
  Thread offset 1 (e.g., T1, T5, T9, ...) -> columns 16-31
  Thread offset 2 (e.g., T2, T6, T10, ...) -> columns 32-47
  Thread offset 3 (e.g., T3, T7, T11, ...) -> columns 48-63

Thread reuse for rows 8-15:
  The same 32 threads (T0-T31) are reused for rows 8-15, but now hold register elements a8 through a15 instead of a0 through a7.
  The row-to-thread mapping is identical: row 8 uses T0-T3, row 9 uses T4-T7, ..., row 15 uses T28-T31.

Diagonal arrows in the figure indicate the continuation of the pattern through the blank rows (rows 2-6 and rows 10-14).

6. REGISTER PACKING
--------------------
Each thread holds 16 register elements total for this fragment (columns 0-63):
  Elements a0 through a7: used for matrix rows 0-7 (upper half)
  Elements a8 through a15: used for matrix rows 8-15 (lower half)

Each column group of 16 columns contributes 16 elements per thread (a0-a7 or a8-a15 depending on row group).
Since the data types are 4-bit (u4/s4) or e2m1, multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register:
  For 4-bit types: 8 elements per 32-bit register (8 x 4 bits = 32 bits)
  Therefore a0-a7 fits in 1 x 32-bit register, and a8-a15 fits in 1 x 32-bit register.
  Total: 2 x 32-bit registers per thread for columns 0-63.

NOTE: This figure shows only columns 0-63. The full k=128 sparse layout would also include columns 64-127 in a separate figure, contributing additional register elements beyond a15.


  Figure 140 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for columns 0–63 of matrix A with `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-A-last64col]
SPARSE MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
Figure: Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for columns 64-127 of matrix A with .u4/.s4/.e2m1 type

==============================================================
1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTIFICATION
==============================================================
Operation: MMA .m16n8k128
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
This figure covers columns 64 through 127 of matrix A (the second quarter of the K dimension in the sparse layout)

==============================================================
2. GRID STRUCTURE
==============================================================
Rows: Matrix rows 0 through 15 (16 rows total, M=16)
Columns: Matrix columns 64 through 127, subdivided into four column ranges:
  - Columns 64...79   (16 columns)
  - Columns 80...95   (16 columns)
  - Columns 96...111  (16 columns)
  - Columns 112...127 (16 columns)

==============================================================
3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (COMPLETE)
==============================================================

ROWS 0-7 (upper half of M dimension):
Each thread holds elements a16 through a23 (8 elements total) for this column range.

Row 0, Columns 64-79:    T0:{a16 ... a23}
Row 0, Columns 80-95:    T1:{a16 ... a23}
Row 0, Columns 96-111:   T2:{a16 ... a23}
Row 0, Columns 112-127:  T3:{a16 ... a23}

Row 1, Columns 64-79:    T4:{a16 ... a23}
Row 1, Columns 80-95:    T5:{a16 ... a23}
Row 1, Columns 96-111:   T6:{a16 ... a23}
Row 1, Columns 112-127:  T7:{a16 ... a23}

Row 2: (implied continuation, cells empty in figure)
...
Row 7, Columns 64-79:    T28:{a16 ... a23}
Row 7, Columns 80-95:    T29:{a16 ... a23}
Row 7, Columns 96-111:   T30:{a16 ... a23}
Row 7, Columns 112-127:  T31:{a16 ... a23}

Pattern for rows 0-7: Thread assignment increments by 4 threads per row.
  Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
  Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
  Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11  (implied)
  Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
  Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
  Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
  Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
  Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

ROWS 8-15 (lower half of M dimension):
Each thread holds elements a24 through a31 (8 elements total) for this column range.

Row 8,  Columns 64-79:    T0:{a24 ... a31}
Row 8,  Columns 80-95:    T1:{a24 ... a31}
Row 8,  Columns 96-111:   T2:{a24 ... a31}
Row 8,  Columns 112-127:  T3:{a24 ... a31}

Row 9,  Columns 64-79:    T4:{a24 ... a31}
Row 9,  Columns 80-95:    T5:{a24 ... a31}
Row 9,  Columns 96-111:   T6:{a24 ... a31}
Row 9,  Columns 112-127:  T7:{a24 ... a31}

Row 10: (implied continuation, cells empty in figure)
...
Row 15, Columns 64-79:    T28:{a24 ... a31}
Row 15, Columns 80-95:    T29:{a24 ... a31}
Row 15, Columns 96-111:   T30:{a24 ... a31}
Row 15, Columns 112-127:  T31:{a24 ... a31}

Pattern for rows 8-15: Same thread assignment as rows 0-7 (T0-T31 in groups of 4 per row),
but elements shift from a16-a23 to a24-a31.

==============================================================
4. ELEMENT NAMING AND REGISTER PACKING
==============================================================
Elements a16 through a23: 8 elements per thread for rows 0-7, columns 64-127
Elements a24 through a31: 8 elements per thread for rows 8-15, columns 64-127

For .u4/.s4 types: each element is 4 bits, so 8 elements = 32 bits = 1 register (32-bit)
For .e2m1 type: each element is 4 bits (2-bit exponent + 1-bit mantissa + 1 sign), so 8 elements = 32 bits = 1 register

Each thread holds 2 groups of 8 elements across the full column range 64-127:
  - a16...a23 covering rows 0-7
  - a24...a31 covering rows 8-15

==============================================================
5. GROUPING PATTERNS SUMMARY
==============================================================
- Threads T0-T3:   Cover all 4 column sub-ranges (64-79, 80-95, 96-111, 112-127) of row 0 (and row 8)
- Threads T4-T7:   Cover all 4 column sub-ranges of row 1 (and row 9)
- Threads T8-T11:  Cover all 4 column sub-ranges of row 2 (and row 10)
- Threads T12-T15: Cover all 4 column sub-ranges of row 3 (and row 11)
- Threads T16-T19: Cover all 4 column sub-ranges of row 4 (and row 12)
- Threads T20-T23: Cover all 4 column sub-ranges of row 5 (and row 13)
- Threads T24-T27: Cover all 4 column sub-ranges of row 6 (and row 14)
- Threads T28-T31: Cover all 4 column sub-ranges of row 7 (and row 15)

Each thread covers two matrix rows (one in 0-7 range, one in 8-15 range) using different element indices (a16-a23 vs a24-a31).

==============================================================
6. ARROWS IN FIGURE
==============================================================
Two diagonal arrows are present in the figure indicating the continuation pattern (implied rows 2-6 and rows 10-14) connecting the visible populated rows to show the repeating thread assignment pattern across the omitted rows.


  Figure 141 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for columns 64–127 of matrix A with `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID            for ai where  0 <= i < 8 || 16 <= i < 24
             groupID + 8        Otherwise

  col = [firstcol ... lastcol]  // As per the mapping of non-zero elements
                                // as described in Sparse matrix storage

  Where
  firstcol = threadID_in_group * 16           For ai where i <  16
             (threadID_in_group * 16) + 64    For ai where i >= 16
  lastcol  = firstcol + 15
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u4` / `.s4` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, each containing eight `.u4` / `.s4` elements from matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3, …, b31 |
  | `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, each containing eight `.e2m1` elements from matrix B. |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 142](#sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-b1),
  [Figure 143](#sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-b2), [Figure 144](#sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-b3), [Figure 145](#sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-b4).

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-B1]
SPARSE MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
Operation: .m16n8k128
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Data types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1
Fragment identifier: %laneid:{fragments}

MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND CONTEXT
M = 16, N = 8, K = 128
This figure shows rows 0-31 of matrix B (the K dimension spans 128 elements; rows 0-31 correspond to the K-dimension rows of B)

GRID STRUCTURE
Rows (outer): Groups of matrix rows, labeled 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31
Columns (register index): 0, 1, 2, ... (ellipsis), 7
Total columns shown: 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 register columns total)

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING TABLE

Row group 0-7 (matrix rows 0 through 7):
  Column 0: T0  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 1: T4  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied, continues pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}

Row group 8-15 (matrix rows 8 through 15):
  Column 0: T1  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 1: T5  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied, continues pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: T29 holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}

Row group 16-23 (matrix rows 16 through 23):
  Column 0: T2  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 1: T6  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied, continues pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: R30 holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  (Note: label shown as "R30" in figure, likely T30)

Row group 24-31 (matrix rows 24 through 31):
  Column 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied, continues pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, .., b6, b7}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
Threads are assigned to row groups as follows:
  Threads T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28 cover matrix rows 0-7
  Threads T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29 cover matrix rows 8-15
  Threads T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30 cover matrix rows 16-23
  Threads T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31 cover matrix rows 24-31

Within each row group, the 8 threads (columns 0 through 7) correspond to threads spaced 4 apart:
  Column 0 = thread base (T0, T1, T2, T3)
  Column 1 = thread base + 4 (T4, T5, T6, T7)
  Column 2 = thread base + 8 (implied)
  ...
  Column 7 = thread base + 28 (T28, T29, T30, T31)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 elements per register column: b0, b1, b2 (shown as ..), b3, b4, b5 (shown as ..), b6, b7
The notation {b0, b1, .., b6, b7} indicates 8 packed elements (b0 through b7) stored in the register(s) for that thread at that column position.
Given the data types are 4-bit (.u4/.s4) or 2-bit (.e2m1), multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register:
  For .u4/.s4: 8 x 4-bit = 32 bits = 1 register per column entry
  For .e2m1: 8 x 2-bit = 16 bits, potentially 2 column entries per register

ARROW IN FIGURE
A diagonal arrow spans from the upper-right area (row group 0-7, column 7 region) down to the lower area (row group 24-31, column 2 region), indicating the continuation of the pattern across the ellipsis columns (columns 2 through 6 are not explicitly shown but follow the same layout).


  Figure 142 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for rows 0–31 of matrix B with `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-B2]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k128 MATRIX B FRAGMENT LAYOUT - ROWS 32-63
Data Types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: sparse mma .m16n8k128
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   This figure covers rows 32-63 of matrix B (the second half of the 64-row B matrix)
   Full matrix B dimensions: 64 rows x 8 columns (columns 0-7)

2. DATA TYPES
   .u4 (unsigned 4-bit integer)
   .s4 (signed 4-bit integer)
   .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa floating point)
   Due to the small element sizes, multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register.

3. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (matrix rows): 32-63, divided into 4 groups of 8 rows each
   Columns: 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 columns total)
   Threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads total, labeled as %laneid:{fragments})
   Note: One entry shows "R30" which appears to be a label variant for T30.

4. THREAD-TO-ROW MAPPING (Row groups)
   Rows 32-39: T0, T4, T8(implied), ..., T28 (threads 0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28)
   Rows 40-47: T1, T5, T9(implied), ..., T29 (threads 1, 5, 9, 13, 17, 21, 25, 29)
   Rows 48-55: T2, T6, T10(implied), ..., T30/R30 (threads 2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22, 26, 30)
   Rows 56-63: T3, T7, T11(implied), ..., T31 (threads 3, 7, 11, 15, 19, 23, 27, 31)

5. COLUMN-TO-THREAD MAPPING
   Column 0: T0 (rows 32-39), T1 (rows 40-47), T2 (rows 48-55), T3 (rows 56-63)
   Column 1: T4 (rows 32-39), T5 (rows 40-47), T6 (rows 48-55), T7 (rows 56-63)
   Column 2: (implied, shown with arrow indicating continuation pattern)
   Columns 3-6: ... (implied continuation)
   Column 7: T28 (rows 32-39), T29 (rows 40-47), R30/T30 (rows 48-55), T31 (rows 56-63)

6. REGISTER ELEMENTS PER THREAD
   Each thread holds a fragment consisting of registers b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   Explicitly shown elements in each thread's fragment:
     b8
     b9
     .. (implied intermediate registers b10, b11, b12, b13)
     b14
     b15
   Total registers per thread for this fragment: 8 registers (b8 through b15)

7. COMPLETE THREAD REGISTER ASSIGNMENT TABLE
   Thread | Matrix Rows | Column | Registers
   T0     | 32-39       | 0      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T1     | 40-47       | 0      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T2     | 48-55       | 0      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T3     | 56-63       | 0      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T4     | 32-39       | 1      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T5     | 40-47       | 1      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T6     | 48-55       | 1      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T7     | 56-63       | 1      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T8-T27 | (rows 32-63)| 2-6    | b8, b9, .., b14, b15 (implied by arrow/ellipsis)
   T28    | 32-39       | 7      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T29    | 40-47       | 7      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T30/R30| 48-55       | 7      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15
   T31    | 56-63       | 7      | b8, b9, .., b14, b15

8. PACKING DETAILS
   For 4-bit types (.u4/.s4): each 32-bit register holds 8 elements packed
   For .e2m1 (3-bit): packing density is approximately 10 elements per 32-bit register
   The registers b8-b15 represent 8 x 32-bit registers per thread for this row range (rows 32-63)
   The arrow in column 2 spanning rows 32-63 indicates the same pattern continues across all 8 columns.

9. NOTATION
   %laneid:{fragments} indicates the fragment assignment is indexed by lane ID (thread index within warp)
   The label "R30" in the rows 48-55, column 7 cell appears to be a typographic variant of T30.


  Figure 143 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for rows 32–63 of matrix B with `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-B3]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k128 FRAGMENT LAYOUT - MATRIX B, ROWS 64-95
Data Types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1

MATRIX IDENTITY:
- Instruction: sparse mma .m16n8k128
- Matrix: B
- Row range shown: 64 through 95
- Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (outer): Matrix rows 64-71, 72-79, 80-87, 88-95 (four bands of 8 rows each)
- Columns: 0, 1, 2, ... 7 (8 columns total, columns 3-6 implied by "...")
- Threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)
- Notation at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

TABLE LAYOUT (Row\Col):

ROW BAND 64-71 (rows 64 through 71):
  Col 0: T0  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 1: T4  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 2: (implied continuation)
  ...
  Col 7: T28 holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}

ROW BAND 72-79 (rows 72 through 79):
  Col 0: T1  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 1: T5  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 2: (implied continuation)
  ...
  Col 7: T29 holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}

ROW BAND 80-87 (rows 80 through 87):
  Col 0: T2  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 1: T6  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 2: (implied continuation)
  ...
  Col 7: R30 holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}

ROW BAND 88-95 (rows 88 through 95):
  Col 0: T3  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 1: T7  holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}
  Col 2: (implied continuation)
  ...
  Col 7: T31 holds {b16, b17, .., b22, b23}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0, T1, T2, T3 handle columns 0 of row bands 64-71, 72-79, 80-87, 88-95 respectively
- Threads T4, T5, T6, T7 handle column 1 of the same four row bands respectively
- This pattern continues: threads T(4k), T(4k+1), T(4k+2), T(4k+3) handle column k for k=0..7
- Thread assignment: column index = thread_id / 4; row band index = thread_id mod 4
- T28, T29, R30 (likely T30), T31 handle column 7 of the four row bands

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING:
- Each thread holds registers containing elements labeled b16, b17, .., b22, b23
- The ".." notation between b17 and b22 indicates elements b18, b19, b20, b21 are also present but not explicitly labeled
- Total elements per thread per cell: 8 elements (b16 through b23)
- These are packed into 32-bit registers; given 4-bit data types (.u4/.s4) or .e2m1, multiple elements pack per register
- For 4-bit types: 8 elements per 32-bit register (8 x 4 bits = 32 bits)
- Elements b16-b23 represent 8 consecutive packed values within the register file

ARROW ANNOTATION:
- A diagonal arrow spans from the upper-right area (col 2, row band 64-71) down to the lower area (col 2, row band 88-95), indicating the column 2 pattern continues through all four row bands in the same manner as shown for columns 0, 1, and 7.


  Figure 144 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for rows 64–95 of matrix B with `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-168128-u4s4-B4]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k128 FRAGMENT LAYOUT - MATRIX B, ROWS 96-127
Data Types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1

OVERVIEW:
- Matrix: B (input matrix)
- MMA dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=128
- This figure covers rows 96 through 127 of matrix B
- Threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads, i.e., one warp)
- Columns shown: 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 columns total)
- Register notation: %laneid:{fragments}

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (of the table) represent groups of matrix rows assigned to thread groups
- Columns (0 through 7) represent register indices within each thread
- Each thread holds registers b24, b25, .., b30, b31 (registers 24 through 31, with intermediate values implied by "..")

THREAD-TO-ROW MAPPING:
Row group 96-103   -> T0  (col 0), T4  (col 1), [col 2 implied], ..., T28 (col 7)
Row group 104-111  -> T1  (col 0), T5  (col 1), [col 2 implied], ..., T29 (col 7)
Row group 112-119  -> T2  (col 0), T6  (col 1), [col 2 implied], ..., R30 (col 7)  [note: labeled R30, likely T30]
Row group 120-127  -> T3  (col 0), T7  (col 1), [col 2 implied], ..., T31 (col 7)

DETAILED REGISTER CONTENTS PER THREAD:

Each thread, regardless of row group or column, holds the following register elements:
  b24
  b25
  ..   (implied intermediate elements b26, b27, b28, b29)
  b30
  b31

This means each thread holds 8 register elements: b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31 (registers indexed 24 through 31).

FULL THREAD REGISTER ASSIGNMENT TABLE:

Thread | Matrix Rows | Col Index | Register Elements
T0     | 96-103      | 0         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T1     | 104-111     | 0         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T2     | 112-119     | 0         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T3     | 120-127     | 0         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T4     | 96-103      | 1         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T5     | 104-111     | 1         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T6     | 112-119     | 1         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T7     | 120-127     | 1         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T8-T27 | (cols 2-6, implied by "...") | 2-6 | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T28    | 96-103      | 7         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T29    | 104-111     | 7         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T30    | 112-119     | 7         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31
T31    | 120-127     | 7         | b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31

PACKING DETAILS:
- Data types .u4, .s4, and .e2m1 are 4-bit types
- Each 32-bit register holds 8 x 4-bit elements
- Registers b24 through b31 = 8 registers x 8 elements = 64 elements per thread per column
- The column index (0-7) corresponds to the N dimension (8 columns of matrix B)
- The row groups (96-103, 104-111, 112-119, 120-127) each span 8 rows, covering K-dimension elements

PATTERN SUMMARY:
- Threads T0-T3 cover columns 0 of rows 96-127 (one thread per 8-row group)
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1 of rows 96-127
- Pattern repeats: threads T(4*col) through T(4*col+3) cover column col
- T28-T31 cover column 7 of rows 96-127
- Each thread stores register indices b24 through b31 (8 registers total)
- The arrow in the figure (pointing from col 2 area diagonally) indicates the continuation pattern across columns 2 through 6 (implied by "...")


  Figure 145 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for rows 96–127 of matrix B with `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.
* Matrix fragments for accumulators C and D are the same as in case of
  [Matrix Fragments for mma.m16n8k64](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16864).
* Metadata: A `.b32` register containing 16 2-bit vectors with each pair of 2-bit vectors storing
  the indices of four non-zero elements from a 8-wide chunk of matrix A as shown in
  [Figure 146](#sparse-mma-metadata-168128-u4s4-first64col) and [Figure 147](#sparse-mma-metadata-168128-u4s4-last64col).

  > 

[Figure: sparse-mma-metadata-168128-u4s4-first64col]
SPARSE MMA METADATA LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
========================================

1. MMA SHAPE AND DATA TYPES
----------------------------
Shape: .m16n8k128
Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
This figure covers columns 0 through 63 (the first half of the k=128 dimension).

2. TABLE STRUCTURE
------------------
The table has rows 0–15 (16 rows total, corresponding to the m=16 dimension) and 8 column groups:
  Columns 0..7
  Columns 8..15
  Columns 16..23
  Columns 24..31
  Columns 32..39
  Columns 40..47
  Columns 48..55
  Columns 56..63

Rows 2–6 and rows 10–14 are omitted with "..." notation, indicating the same pattern continues.

3. THREAD ASSIGNMENTS AND BIT POSITIONS
-----------------------------------------
The notation is: T<thread_index>: [high_bit..low_bit]

The thread index uses a parameterized variable "i" where i ranges to cover all 32 threads in a warp.

ROW 0:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i}:     [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i}:     [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i}:     [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i}:     [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i}:     [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i}:     [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i}:     [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i}:     [31..28]

ROW 1:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+4}:   [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+4}:   [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+4}:   [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+4}:   [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+4}:   [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+4}:   [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+4}:   [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+4}:   [31..28]

ROW 7:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+28}:  [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+28}:  [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+28}:  [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+28}:  [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+28}:  [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+28}:  [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+28}:  [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+28}:  [31..28]

ROW 8:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+1}:   [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+1}:   [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+1}:   [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+1}:   [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+1}:   [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+1}:   [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+1}:   [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+1}:   [31..28]

ROW 9:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+5}:   [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+5}:   [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+5}:   [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+5}:   [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+5}:   [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+5}:   [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+5}:   [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+5}:   [31..28]

ROW 15:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+29}:  [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+29}:  [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+29}:  [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+29}:  [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+29}:  [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+29}:  [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+29}:  [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+29}:  [31..28]

4. METADATA ENCODING FORMAT
-----------------------------
Each thread holds a 32-bit metadata register. The 32 bits are divided into eight 4-bit fields:
  Bits [3..0]   -> metadata for columns 0..7
  Bits [7..4]   -> metadata for columns 8..15
  Bits [11..8]  -> metadata for columns 16..23
  Bits [15..12] -> metadata for columns 24..31
  Bits [19..16] -> metadata for columns 32..39
  Bits [23..20] -> metadata for columns 40..47
  Bits [27..24] -> metadata for columns 48..55
  Bits [31..28] -> metadata for columns 56..63

Each 4-bit field encodes the sparsity selection for 8 consecutive columns (one group of 8 elements). For 2:4 structured sparsity at 4-bit (u4/s4) or e2m1 precision, each group of 4 elements has 2 non-zero values selected by 2-bit indices, so 4 bits cover 2 selections of 2 bits each within the 8-column group.

5. THREAD GROUPING AND ROW PATTERNS
--------------------------------------
Rows 0–7 (upper half of m=16):
  Row 0 uses thread T_{2i}
  Row 1 uses thread T_{2i+4}
  Row 2 uses thread T_{2i+8}   (implied by pattern)
  Row 3 uses thread T_{2i+12}  (implied)
  Row 4 uses thread T_{2i+16}  (implied)
  Row 5 uses thread T_{2i+20}  (implied)
  Row 6 uses thread T_{2i+24}  (implied)
  Row 7 uses thread T_{2i+28}
  -> Thread offset increments by +4 per row for rows 0–7, using even-indexed base threads (T_{2i})

Rows 8–15 (lower half of m=16):
  Row 8  uses thread T_{2i+1}
  Row 9  uses thread T_{2i+5}
  Row 10 uses thread T_{2i+9}  (implied)
  Row 11 uses thread T_{2i+13} (implied)
  Row 12 uses thread T_{2i+17} (implied)
  Row 13 uses thread T_{2i+21} (implied)
  Row 14 uses thread T_{2i+25} (implied)
  Row 15 uses thread T_{2i+29}
  -> Thread offset increments by +4 per row for rows 8–15, using odd-indexed base threads (T_{2i+1})

SUMMARY OF KEY PATTERNS:
- Each thread holds exactly 32 bits of metadata covering all 8 column groups (0..63) for a single row.
- The 32-bit register is fully consumed by the 8 column groups (8 groups x 4 bits = 32 bits).
- Rows 0–7 are handled by even-offset threads (2i, 2i+4, ..., 2i+28).
- Rows 8–15 are handled by odd-offset threads (2i+1, 2i+5, ..., 2i+29).
- The parameter i indexes pairs of threads, stepping through the warp in groups of 2.
- This figure covers only columns 0–63; a companion figure covers columns 64–127 for the full k=128 extent.

  >
  > Figure 146 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 metadata layout for columns 0–63 for `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.

==============================================================
3. COMPLETE THREAD AND BIT ASSIGNMENTS (ALL CELLS)
==============================================================

ROW 2:

ROWS 3..6: (indicated by "..." — blank/implied continuation)

ROW 7:
  Columns 64..71:   T30: [3..0]
  Columns 72..79:   T30: [7..4]
  Columns 80..87:   T30: [11..8]
  Columns 88..95:   T30: [15..12]

  Columns 72..79:   T31: [7..4]

==============================================================
4. THREAD NUMBERING PATTERN
==============================================================
Rows 0-7 (upper half of M=16):
  Row 0:  Thread T2
  Row 1:  Thread T6
  Row 2:  Thread T10 (implied by pattern)
  Row 3:  Thread T14 (implied)
  Row 4:  Thread T18 (implied)
  Row 5:  Thread T22 (implied)
  Row 6:  Thread T26 (implied)
  Row 7:  Thread T30

Thread stride in upper half: +4 per row (T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30)

Rows 8-15 (lower half of M=16):
  Row 8:  Thread T3
  Row 9:  Thread T7
  Row 10: Thread T11 (implied)
  Row 11: Thread T15 (implied)
  Row 12: Thread T19 (implied)
  Row 13: Thread T23 (implied)
  Row 14: Thread T27 (implied)
  Row 15: Thread T31

Thread stride in lower half: +4 per row (T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31)

Upper half uses even-offset threads starting at T2 (mod 4 = 2)
Lower half uses odd-offset threads starting at T3 (mod 4 = 3)

==============================================================
5. BIT FIELD ASSIGNMENTS WITHIN EACH THREAD'S 32-BIT REGISTER
==============================================================
Each thread holds a 32-bit metadata register. The bits are distributed across the 8 column groups as follows:

Column group 64..71:   bits [3..0]   (4 bits)
Column group 72..79:   bits [7..4]   (4 bits)
Column group 80..87:   bits [11..8]  (4 bits)
Column group 88..95:   bits [15..12] (4 bits)
Column group 96..103:  bits [19..16] (4 bits)
Column group 104..111: bits [23..20] (4 bits)
Column group 112..119: bits [27..24] (4 bits)
Column group 120..127: bits [31..28] (4 bits)

Total: 8 groups x 4 bits = 32 bits per thread register

Each 4-bit field covers 8 columns. Within each 8-column span, 2-bit sub-fields encode which 2 of 4 elements are non-zero (2:4 structured sparsity). Two 2-bit selectors per 4-bit field = 2 pairs of elements selected from 4.

==============================================================
6. METADATA ENCODING FORMAT
==============================================================
For .u4/.s4/.e2m1 types with k=128 and 2:4 sparsity:
- Every group of 4 consecutive elements along the K dimension has exactly 2 non-zero values
- Each 2-bit selector encodes which 2 of 4 elements are retained
- 4 bits per 8-column group encodes two consecutive 2:4 groups (columns i..i+3 and i+4..i+7)
- The metadata register for each thread is 32 bits wide, covering all 8 column groups (64 columns total for this half)
- Combined with the companion figure (columns 0-63), the full 32-bit metadata register covers all k=128 columns

==============================================================
7. DIAGONAL ARROW ANNOTATIONS
==============================================================
Two diagonal arrows are visible in the figure:
Arrow 1: Points from the area of row 2 / column 88..95 leftward-downward toward row 7 / column 72..79, indicating the continuation of the thread pattern (T10 through T30) fills rows 2-7.
Arrow 2: Points from the area of row 10 / column 104..111 rightward-upward toward row 10 / column 120..127, indicating the continuation of the thread pattern (T11 through T31) fills rows 10-15.
These arrows visually indicate that the blank rows follow the same thread-increment-by-4 pattern as the explicitly shown rows.

==============================================================
8. RELATIONSHIP TO FULL k=128 LAYOUT
==============================================================
This figure covers columns 64-127 (the second 64 columns of k=128).
The companion figure covers columns 0-63 (the first 64 columns).
Together, both figures define the complete 32-bit metadata register content for each thread.
Bits [31..0] of each thread's metadata register are split: bits for columns 0-63 come from the first figure, bits for columns 64-127 come from this figure, with this figure assigning bits [31..0

##### 9.7.14.6.3. [Multiply-and-Accumulate Instruction: `mma.sp` / `mma.sp::ordered_metadata`](#warp-level-matrix-instructions-sparse-mma)

`mma.sp`, `mma.sp::ordered_metadata`

Perform matrix multiply-and-accumulate operation with sparse matrix A

Syntax

Half precision floating point type:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.dtype.f16.f16.ctype  d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k32.row.col.dtype.f16.f16.ctype  d, a, b, c, e, f;

.ctype     = {.f16, .f32};
.dtype     = {.f16, .f32};
.spvariant = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};
```

Alternate floating point type:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.bf16.bf16.f32     d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k32.row.col.f32.bf16.bf16.f32     d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k8.row.col.f32.tf32.tf32.f32      d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.tf32.tf32.f32     d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.f32.f8type.f8type.f32 d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.sp::ordered_metadata.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.kind.dtype.f8f6f4type.f8f6f4type.ctype d, a, b, c, e, f;

.f8type     = {.e4m3, .e5m2};
.spvariant  = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};
.f8f6f4type = {.e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1};
.kind       = {kind::f8f6f4};
.ctype      = {.f16, .f32};
.dtype      = {.f16, .f32};
```

Alternate floating point type with block scaling:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k128.row.col.kind.block_scale{.scale_vec_size}.f32.e2m1.e2m1.f32.stype d, a, b, c, e, f, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.spvariant      = {.sp::ordered_metadata};
.kind           = {.kind::mxf4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::2X};
.stype          = {.ue8m0};

mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k128.row.col.kind.block_scale.scale_vec_size.f32.e2m1.e2m1.f32.stype d, a, b, c, e, f, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.spvariant      = {.sp::ordered_metadata};
.kind           = {.kind::mxf4nvf4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::2X, .scale_vec::4X};
.stype          = {.ue8m0, .ue4m3};

mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.kind.block_scale{.scale_vec_size}.f32.f8f6f4type.f8f6f4type.f32.stype d, a, b, c, e, f, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.spvariant      = {.sp::ordered_metadata};
.kind           = {.kind::mxf8f6f4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::1X};
.f8f6f4type     = {.e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1};
.stype          = {.ue8m0};
```

Integer type:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.shape.row.col{.satfinite}.s32.atype.btype.s32 d, a, b, c, e, f;

.shape     = {.m16n8k32, .m16n8k64}
.atype     = {.u8, .s8};
.btype     = {.u8, .s8};
.spvariant = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};

mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.shape.row.col{.satfinite}.s32.atype.btype.s32 d, a, b, c, e, f;

.shape     = {.m16n8k64, .m16n8k128}
.atype     = {.u4, .s4};
.btype     = {.u4, .s4};
.spvariant = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};
```

Description

Perform a `MxNxK` matrix multiply and accumulate operation, `D = A*B+C`, where the A matrix is
`MxK`, the B matrix is `KxN`, and the C and D matrices are `MxN`.

A warp executing `mma.sp.sync/mma.sp::ordered_metadata.sync` instruction compute a single matrix
multiply and accumulate operation.

Qualifier `.block_scale` specifies that the matrices `A` and `B` are scaled with `scale_A`
and `scale_B` matrices respectively before performing the matrix multiply and accumulate operation
as specified in the section [Block Scaling for mma.sync](#warp-level-block-scaling). The data type corresponding
to each of the element within `scale_A` and `scale_B` matrices is specified by `.stype`.
Qualifier `.scale_vec_size` specifies the number of columns of `scale_A` matrix and number of
rows in the matrix `scale_B`.
